q-oni-oniannexe2fig10-ff141136-da02f7af.stem: At night, which lights must a sailing vessel under motor (sails hoisted or not) carry?
choices: A white masthead light, green and red side lights, and a white stern light
Only an ordinary white light visible from all sides
A white masthead light and a tricolour light combining the side lights and the stern light at the top of the mast
q-oni-oniannexe2fig10-ff141136-da02f7af.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 10 — white masthead light, green/red side lights, white stern light.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig11-a7ce07dd-021a8527.stem: A sailing vessel under motor may combine its side lights and its stern light into a single tricolour light. Where is this tricolour light placed, and which other light is still required?
choices: A white masthead light, with the tricolour light placed at the top of the mast or near it
A white masthead light, with the tricolour light placed on the stern at water level
No masthead light, and a white stern light separate from the tricolour light
q-oni-oniannexe2fig11-a7ce07dd-021a8527.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 11 — white masthead light; side lights and stern light combined into a tricolour light at the top of the mast or near it.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig14-45636e4f-f71e248a.stem: At night, which light must a non-motorised vessel sailing alone or in a towed convoy carry?
choices: An ordinary white light visible from all sides
A white masthead light with green and red side lights
An ordinary yellow light visible from all sides
q-oni-oniannexe2fig14-45636e4f-f71e248a.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 14 — non-motorised vessels, alone or towed: ordinary white light visible from all sides.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig15-42ccc9b1-20822384.stem: At night, which light must a vessel under sail (without motor) carry as a minimum?
choices: An ordinary white light visible from all sides
Green and red side lights and a white stern light
A white masthead light and a tricolour light at the top of the mast
q-oni-oniannexe2fig15-42ccc9b1-20822384.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 15 — vessels under sail: ordinary white light visible from all sides.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig17-77b94b84-cb761fb4.stem: What does this light signal mean?
choices: Flashing light
Fixed light visible from all sides
Tricolour light placed at the top of the mast or near it
q-oni-oniannexe2fig17-77b94b84-cb761fb4.explanation: ONI Annexe 2 – fig. 17 — « Tricolour light placed at the top of the mast or near it ».

q-oni-oniannexe2fig18-ec6e52d1-c0d19d8f.stem: At night, a vessel carries two superimposed lights visible from all sides, a stern light and a bicolour light (or separate side lights). What are the colours of the two superimposed lights?
choices: The upper light red, the lower light green
The upper light green, the lower light red
Both lights white
q-oni-oniannexe2fig18-ec6e52d1-c0d19d8f.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 18 — two superimposed lights visible from all sides: the upper red, the lower green.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig19-afd9bcc6-74e76f73.stem: At night, which light must a vessel at anchor carry?
choices: An ordinary white light visible from all sides
An ordinary red light visible from all sides
Green and red side lights
q-oni-oniannexe2fig19-afd9bcc6-74e76f73.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 19 — vessels at anchor: ordinary white light visible from all sides.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig20-188a22fa-4dc0d6cb.stem: At night, when the safety of navigation requires it, how must floating equipment be marked?
choices: By lighting that allows its outline to be made out
By an ordinary yellow light visible from all sides
By a white masthead light and green and red side lights
q-oni-oniannexe2fig20-188a22fa-4dc0d6cb.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 20 — floating equipment: when safety requires it, lighting that allows the outline to be made out.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig21-a4c59f38-15b5ccdf.stem: In addition to the usual navigation lights, which distinctive light must a priority vessel carry at night?
choices: A bright green light visible from all sides, placed at least 1 m higher than the masthead light
A bright red light visible from all sides, placed at least 1 m higher than the masthead light
An ordinary yellow light visible from all sides
q-oni-oniannexe2fig21-a4c59f38-15b5ccdf.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 21 — priority vessels: a bright green light visible from all sides, at least 1 m above the masthead light.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig23-78f17195-0b4083e1.stem: To indicate that it must be protected against wash, which additional lights does a vessel carry at night, in addition to the prescribed lights?
choices: An ordinary red light visible from all sides above an ordinary white light visible from all sides
An ordinary white light above an ordinary red light, both visible from all sides
Two superimposed ordinary red lights visible from all sides
q-oni-oniannexe2fig23-78f17195-0b4083e1.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 23 — protection against wash: red light visible from all sides above a white light visible from all sides.

choices: A flag whose upper half is red and lower half white
Swinging a red flag
Two superimposed white flags
q-oni-oniannexe2fig24-7c82046a-6efffa35.explanation: ONI Annexe 2 – fig. 24 — « A flag whose upper half is red and lower half white ».

choices: Two flags, the upper red and the lower white
Two superimposed white flags
Swinging a red flag
q-oni-oniannexe2fig25-727c3760-bf08969e.explanation: ONI Annexe 2 – fig. 25 — « Two flags, the upper red and the lower white ».

q-oni-oniannexe2fig32-8474c209-75ed07a8.stem: Which signal does a vessel of the police, the border guard or the fisheries inspectorate hoist when it wants to make contact with another vessel?
choices: The letter «K» flag, whose half on the staff side is yellow and the other half blue
The letter «K» flag, whose upper half is red and the other half white
An entirely red flag
q-oni-oniannexe2fig32-8474c209-75ed07a8.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 32 — letter «K» flag, half on the staff side yellow, the other half blue.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig33-f3928ea5-eda240bd.stem: At night, which light must a professional fishing vessel carry?
choices: An ordinary yellow light visible from all sides
An ordinary white light visible from all sides
An ordinary red light visible from all sides
q-oni-oniannexe2fig33-f3928ea5-eda240bd.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 33 — professional fishing vessels: ordinary yellow light visible from all sides.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig35-49c140b6-402a00cf.stem: Which daytime signal must a vessel trolling for fish display?
choices: A white ball
A green ball visible from all sides
An ordinary yellow light visible from all sides
q-oni-oniannexe2fig35-49c140b6-402a00cf.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 35 — vessels trolling for fish: white ball.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig36-f348b5a7-e9b8bb56.stem: In the case of underwater diving carried out from the shore, which signal must be displayed?
choices: The letter «A» board (a swallow-tailed pennant, half on the staff side white, the other half blue)
The letter «K» flag (half on the staff side yellow, the other half blue)
A white ball visible from all sides
q-oni-oniannexe2fig36-f348b5a7-e9b8bb56.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 36 — diving from the shore: letter «A» board, white/blue swallow-tailed pennant.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig37-6c099ecd-a0366ec7.stem: In the case of underwater diving carried out from a body of water, how must the letter «A» board be displayed?
choices: The letter «A» board (white/blue pennant) visible from all sides
The letter «A» board facing only towards the shore
A red flag visible from all sides
q-oni-oniannexe2fig37-6c099ecd-a0366ec7.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 37 — diving from a body of water: letter «A» board (white/blue pennant) visible from all sides.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig4-6f7c0dce-5b9aefe1.stem: At night, which lights must a motorised vessel sailing alone (or a tug) carry?
choices: A bright white masthead (or bow) light, bright green and bright red side lights, and an ordinary white stern light
Only an ordinary white light visible from all sides
A bright white light and a bright green light visible from all sides placed higher than the masthead light
q-oni-oniannexe2fig4-6f7c0dce-5b9aefe1.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 4 — motorised vessels alone/tugs: bright white masthead light, bright green/red side lights, ordinary white stern light.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig40-dc3b2f2d-60fa671a.stem: At night, how does a vessel in distress signal its situation?
choices: By swinging a light in a circular motion
By swinging a light (without a circular motion)
By showing two fixed superimposed red lights
q-oni-oniannexe2fig40-dc3b2f2d-60fa671a.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 40 — vessels in distress: swing a light in a circular motion.

choices: Swinging a red flag
Swinging a red flag, or any other suitable object, in a circular motion
Two superimposed white flags
q-oni-oniannexe2fig41-043d783d-d5fd032a.explanation: ONI Annexe 2 – fig. 41 — « Swinging a red flag, or any other suitable object, in a circular motion ».

choices: A slow, repeated up-and-down movement of the arms extended to each side
Permission to moor
B. in the indicated direction only
q-oni-oniannexe2fig42-f759937d-ad760066.explanation: ONI Annexe 2 – fig. 42 — « A slow, repeated up-and-down movement of the arms extended to each side ».

q-oni-oniannexe2fig43-f7b06d9e-947594fe.stem: At night, how does a piece of floating equipment (or a vessel at work, aground or sunk) indicate the sides on which passage is clear?
choices: By an ordinary red light and an ordinary white light
By an ordinary red light alone
By an ordinary green light and an ordinary white light
q-oni-oniannexe2fig43-f7b06d9e-947594fe.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 43 — side where passage is clear: ordinary red light + ordinary white light; side not clear: ordinary red light alone.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig44-1e963c8a-41be78c5.stem: By day, which flag indicates the side on which passage is NOT clear near a piece of floating equipment or a vessel at work?
choices: A red flag
A flag whose upper half is red and lower half white
An entirely white flag
q-oni-oniannexe2fig44-1e963c8a-41be78c5.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 44 — side where passage is not clear: red flag; side clear: red/white flag.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig45-46d6d361-4c73921b.stem: By day, which signal indicates the side on which passage is clear near a piece of floating equipment or a vessel at work (two-flag variant)?
choices: Two superimposed flags, the upper red and the lower white
Two superimposed flags, the upper white and the lower red
A single red flag
q-oni-oniannexe2fig45-46d6d361-4c73921b.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 45 — side clear: two superimposed flags, upper red, lower white; side not clear: red flag.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig5-b9cc2457-0d1812fc.stem: At night, where is the masthead light (bright white light) of a pushed convoy placed?
choices: On the leading vessel
On the pushing vessel, at the rear of the convoy
At the centre of the convoy, on the most visible vessel
q-oni-oniannexe2fig5-b9cc2457-0d1812fc.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 5 — pushed convoys: bright white masthead light placed on the leading vessel.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig8-abe2e33a-1a2b7850.stem: At night, which lights may a professional fishing, sport or motorised pleasure vessel (or a sailing vessel under motor) carry under this simplified variant?
choices: A white light visible from all sides and green and red side lights
A white masthead light, green/red side lights and a separate white stern light
Only an ordinary white light visible from all sides
q-oni-oniannexe2fig8-abe2e33a-1a2b7850.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 8 — white light visible from all sides + green and red side lights.

q-oni-oniannexe2fig9-ea35d966-a3f6fbb6.stem: Under this variant, how may the side lights of a motorised sport/pleasure vessel (or sailing vessel under motor) carrying a white light visible from all sides be arranged in the forward area?
choices: Side by side in the forward area, or as a bicolour light
Combined with the stern light into a tricolour light at the top of the mast
Replaced by a single ordinary yellow light visible from all sides
q-oni-oniannexe2fig9-ea35d966-a3f6fbb6.explanation: ONI Annexe 2, fig. 9 — white light visible from all sides + green/red side lights, placed side by side in the forward area or as a bicolour light.

q-oni-oniannexe4fig1-9fc6fa6d-38447aa2.stem: What does the general prohibition signal (circular board with a red border and a red bar) placed on a body of water mean?
choices: No passage
The channel is limited to a certain distance from the shore
Recommended passage for crossing under a bridge
q-oni-oniannexe4fig1-9fc6fa6d-38447aa2.explanation: ONI Annexe 4, fig. 1 — general prohibition signal: no passage.

choices: No creating harmful wash or suction effects
No anchoring
No turning
q-oni-oniannexe4fig13-75b0c2e2-13c94af6.explanation: ONI Annexe 4 – fig. 13 — « No creating harmful wash or suction effects ».

choices: No turning
No mooring
No navigating outside the indicated limits
q-oni-oniannexe4fig14-88869f44-6d01af6c.explanation: ONI Annexe 4 – fig. 14 — « No navigating outside the indicated limits ».

choices: No mooring
No windsurfing
No navigating outside the indicated limits
q-oni-oniannexe4fig15-43775bef-5b86f15e.explanation: ONI Annexe 4 – fig. 15 — « No navigating outside the indicated limits ».

choices: Passage prohibited, but prepare to get under way
Place for landing vessels
Wailing of a siren
q-oni-oniannexe4fig16-62fc9542-cac17bfa.explanation: ONI Annexe 4 – fig. 16 — « Passage prohibited, but prepare to get under way ».

choices: Obligation to keep a particular lookout
Obligation to take the direction indicated by the arrow
Obligation to sound a whistle
q-oni-oniannexe4fig18-646acf50-c67c403e.explanation: ONI Annexe 4 – fig. 18 — « Obligation to take the direction indicated by the arrow ».

choices: Obligation not to exceed the speed indicated in km/h
Obligation to keep a particular lookout
Obligation to stop under certain conditions
q-oni-oniannexe4fig20-cfb9895c-05e82069.explanation: ONI Annexe 4 – fig. 20 — « Obligation not to exceed the speed indicated in km/h ».

q-oni-oniannexe4fig25-2aad3948-650cf03e.stem: On the « The channel is limited » signal, what does the number shown on the board represent?
choices: The distance, in metres, at which vessels must keep clear of the shore
The maximum permitted speed in km/h
The minimum water depth in metres
q-oni-oniannexe4fig25-2aad3948-650cf03e.explanation: ONI Annexe 4, fig. 25 — limited channel: the number indicates in metres the distance to keep from the shore.

q-oni-oniannexe4fig27-ba251a55-5d179299.stem: What does the « Recommended passage under bridges » board of type a indicate?
choices: A recommended passage for crossing under the bridge in both directions
A recommended passage in one direction only
A prohibition on crossing under the bridge
q-oni-oniannexe4fig27-ba251a55-5d179299.explanation: ONI Annexe 4, fig. 27 — recommended passage under bridges, a: in both directions.

choices: Obligation to stop under certain conditions
Recommendation to keep within the space indicated in «green»
Permission to pass
q-oni-oniannexe4fig30-76f938fc-a2f82f94.explanation: ONI Annexe 4 – fig. 30 — « Recommendation to keep within the space indicated in «green» ».

choices: No meeting or overtaking of any kind
Recommendation to keep within the space indicated in «green»
End of a prohibition or an obligation
q-oni-oniannexe4fig31-26ad5785-7713c27a.explanation: ONI Annexe 4 – fig. 31 — « Recommendation to keep within the space indicated in «green» ».
